input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                               FontSize="16"
                               VerticalOptions="Center" />
                        <Label Grid.Column="1"
                               Text="›"
                               FontSize="20"
                               VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>

    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;          // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}?id={SelectedItem.Id}");

        SelectedItem = null;           // clears highlight for back-nav
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem → SelectedItem` | Two-way; lets the VM react to selection and clear it |
| `SelectionChangedCommand → ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires navigation when a row is tapped |
| `RefreshView.Command → RefreshCommand` | Triggers API reload on pull-down |
| `IsRefreshing → IsRefreshing` | Two-way; set `false` in VM to dismiss the spinner |

**Things to watch out for:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the row isn't still highlighted when the user comes back.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of `Refresh()` — the `RefreshView` won't stop spinning on its own.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` is required; the default is `None`, which disables selection entirely.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI CollectionView example with single selection and pull-to-refresh, along with XAML and ViewModel explanation. This is a straightforward knowledge question — no codebase exploration needed.
